mnemonics.title: Guided Media Types
mnemonics.content: TUC - Twisted pair, Coaxial cable, and Fiber Optic cable are the three main guided media types

mnemonics.title: Unguided Media Types
mnemonics.content: RIMS - Radio waves, Infrared, Microwave, Satellite - the four unguided transmission types

mnemonics.title: Twisted Pair Cable Types
mnemonics.content: UST - Unshielded Twisted (UTP) and Shielded Twisted (STP) pair cables

mnemonics.title: Fiber Optic Advantages
mnemonics.content: FANS - Faster, Immune to EM interference, No signal loss, Secure - remember FANS keep you cool

mnemonics.title: Coaxial Cable Usage
mnemonics.content: CTV - Cable TV, Television, and VCR connections historically used coaxial cable

acronyms.usage: Most common Ethernet cable, used in LANs, susceptible to interference
Twisted pair with shielding, used in environments with high EMI
Disturbance caused by external electromagnetic fields, affects guided cables
Required for microwave and infrared communication, no obstacles between sender and receiver
Frequency unit for microwave and wireless signals, higher GHz = more data but shorter range

visualTips.visualization: Imagine guided media as water flowing through pipes (physical path), while unguided media is like sunlight traveling through air (no pipe needed)
Picture two wires twisted around each other like a DNA helix - the twisting cancels out electromagnetic interference
Visualize a central copper wire surrounded by insulation, then a braided metal shield, all inside an outer jacket - like a miniature underground tunnel
Imagine light traveling through a glass pipe by total internal reflection, bouncing like a laser beam through a curved tunnel
Picture two mountain peaks with antennas facing each other - Line of Sight communication across the horizon
Visualize a satellite in space acting as a mirror in the sky, reflecting signals between two ground stations on Earth

analogies.analogy: Think of guided media like a railway track - trains (data) must stay on the physical rails (cables)
Like a radio station broadcasting music through the air - anyone with a receiver can pick it up
Like two people rowing a boat in opposite directions - their forces cancel out, reducing waves in the water (EM interference)
Like comparing a motorcycle courier vs a fiber optic signal - light travels at 200,000 km/s, the fastest possible communication
Like using a flashlight to signal someone on another hill - you need clear visibility, obstacles block the signal
Like a shielded microphone cable - the extra protection prevents outside noise from ruining the audio quality
